name: LangChain.js
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); pay only for LLM API calls; LangSmith tracing optional ($39/mo+)

auth.notes: Framework; configure LLM provider API keys in environment; LangSmith tracing uses separate key

performance.notes: Framework overhead minimal; all latency from LLM calls and tool execution

agent_readiness.notes: JavaScript/TypeScript port of LangChain. LCEL (LangChain Expression Language) for composable chains. Agents, RAG, memory abstractions. LangGraph.js for graph-based agentic flows. LangSmith integration for observability. 50+ LLM provider integrations. Feature parity with Python LangChain improving but still behind in some areas.

gotchas: Python LangChain always leads in features — JS version lags by weeks to months
Rapid API changes; frequently breaking between versions
Complex abstraction layers make debugging hard
Bundle size significant for browser/edge deployments
best_when: Next.js / Node.js agent applications where Python isn't an option
Teams using LangChain Python wanting JS parity for frontend
LangGraph.js for graph-based agent orchestration in TypeScript
avoid_when: Can use Python (Python LangChain is more feature-complete)
Simple LLM calls without chaining (overkill — use SDK directly)
Teams wanting stable long-term APIs (frequent breaking changes)
summary: LangChain.js is the JavaScript/TypeScript port of LangChain. LCEL for composable chains, LangGraph.js for graph-based agents, LangSmith for observability. 50+ LLM provider integrations. Full async/streaming support. Best for TypeScript/Node.js backends wanting LangChain ecosystem. Python LangChain leads in features; JS version lags but is improving rapidly. Frequent breaking changes are a known issue.
